mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is useful for ensuring the security of your applications by scanning images and workloads for known vulnerabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configuration and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm charts repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a TrivyConfiguration custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that configures the Trivy Operator's scanning behavior.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aqua.security/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
  severity: HIGH
EOF

```

> Creates a default TrivyConfiguration resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: aqua.security/v1
kind: TrivyConfiguration
metadata:
  name: default
  namespace: trivy-system
spec:
  image:
    repository: aquasec/trivy
    tag: 0.23.0
  severity: HIGH
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify the installation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the TrivyConfiguration and any scan results.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace, which will remove all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your TrivyConfiguration. This step includes draining the operator if necessary, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up the current TrivyConfiguration.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the new version.
> Verifies that the Trivy Operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivyconfiguration-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'trivy-operator pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' and ensure RBAC permissions are correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'TrivyConfiguration not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The TrivyConfiguration resource may not have been created or is in a different namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the TrivyConfiguration is created in the 'trivy-system' namespace with 'kubectl get trivyconfiguration -n trivy-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the Trivy server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be able to reach the Trivy image repository.

**Fix:**
Check network policies and ensure that the Trivy image repository is accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No scan results found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not have scanned any images or workloads yet.

**Fix:**
Ensure that workloads are deployed and that the TrivyOperator is configured to scan them.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom image repositories and improved scanning performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.22, no specific migration steps are required.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to confirm.
codeSnippets.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the created VulnerabilityReport should show a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
